title: Verses - Written Under The Portrait Of Fergusson, The Poet, In A Copy Of That Author's Works Presented To A Young Lady.
content:
Curse on ungrateful man, that can be pleas'd,
        And yet can starve the author of the pleasure!
        O thou my elder brother in misfortune,
        By far my elder brother in the muses,
        With tears I pity thy unhappy fate!
        Why is the bard unpitied by the world,
        Yet has so keen a relish of its pleasures?
meaning:
"Verses - Written Under The Portrait Of Fergusson, The Poet, In A Copy Of That Author's Works Presented To A Young Lady." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
